TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == balu == ŤäĪ manamuh kaku tu kalubalu amin. siwkay nu balu balu sa , mahiza ngangan nu Lipun "hana". yadah ku kulit nu balu, sisa bangcal ku balu. maydih kami tu balu. u Sakizaya a lisin sa, izaw ku "Baluinana", sapibetik tu balu, u mapalaway a lisin kuyza. anu patuduay micumud tu mapalaway a selal atu cacudadan , misaungay tu ku Baluinana a lisin. anu sibaluay a kakitizaan, tayni tu ku wadu atu adipapang. tinaku a kamu balubaluan ŤäĪŚúíÔľõŤäĪśĶ∑ balubaluan ku umah nu Maibul. kasibaluan ťĖčŤäĪÁöĄŚ≠£ÁĮÄ kasibaluan nu mami ayza a bulad. misabalu ŚźęŤčěŚĺÖśĒĺ misabalu tu ku mami. sibalu śúČŤäĪ sibalu tu ku bicacay. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A flower, sometimes known as a bloom or blossom, is the reproductive structure found in plants that are floral (plants of the division Magnoliophyta, also called angiosperms). The biological function of a flower is to effect reproduction, usually by providing a mechanism for the union of sperm with eggs. Flowers may facilitate outcrossing or allow selfing . Some flowers produce diaspores without fertilization . Flowers contain sporangia and are the site where gametophytes develop. Many flowers have evolved to be attractive to animals, so as to cause them to be vectors for the transfer of pollen. After fertilization, the ovary of the flower develops into fruit containing seeds.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺu == * manamuńß kaku tu kar~…ĺubar~…ĺu amin. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺu bar~…ĺu sa , mańßiza ŇčaŇčan nu r~…ĺipun "ńßana". jadańß ku kur~…ĺit nu bar~…ĺu, sisa baŇč ¶ar~…ĺ ku bar~…ĺu. majdińß kami tu bar~…ĺu. u sakizaja a r~…ĺisin sa, izaw ku "bar~…ĺuinana", sapib…ôtik tu bar~…ĺu, u mapar~…ĺawaj a r~…ĺisin kujza. anu patuduaj mi ¶umud tu mapar~…ĺawaj a s…ôr~…ĺar~…ĺ atu ¶a ¶udadan , misauŇčaj tu ku bar~…ĺuinana a r~…ĺisin. anu sibar~…ĺuaj a kakitizaan, tajni tu ku wadu atu adipapaŇč. tinaku a kamu bar~…ĺubar~…ĺuan ***** bar~…ĺubar~…ĺuan ku umańß nu maibur~…ĺ. kasibar~…ĺuan ***** kasibar~…ĺuan nu mami ajza a bur~…ĺad. misabar~…ĺu **** misabar~…ĺu tu ku mami. sibar~…ĺu ** sibar~…ĺu tu ku bi ¶a ¶aj. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a fr~…ĺow…ôr, som…ôtim…ôs known as a br~…ĺoom or br~…ĺossom, is tńß…ô r…ôprodu ¶ti*…ô stru ¶tur…ô found in pr~…ĺants tńßat ar…ô fr~…ĺorar~…ĺ (pr~…ĺants of tńß…ô di*ision magnor~…ĺiopńßjta, ar~…ĺso ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd aŇčiosp…ôrms). tńß…ô bior~…ĺogi ¶ar~…ĺ fun ¶tion of a fr~…ĺow…ôr is to …ôff…ô ¶t r…ôprodu ¶tion, usuar~…ĺr~…ĺj bj pro*idiŇč a m…ô ¶ńßanism for tńß…ô union of sp…ôrm witńß …ôggs. fr~…ĺow…ôrs maj fa ¶ir~…ĺitat…ô out ¶rossiŇč or ar~…ĺr~…ĺow s…ôr~…ĺfiŇč . som…ô fr~…ĺow…ôrs produ ¶…ô diaspor…ôs witńßout f…ôrtir~…ĺization . fr~…ĺow…ôrs ¶ontain sporaŇčia and ar…ô tńß…ô sit…ô wńß…ôr…ô gam…ôtopńßjt…ôs d…ô*…ôr~…ĺop. manj fr~…ĺow…ôrs ńßa*…ô …ô*or~…ĺ*…ôd to b…ô attra ¶ti*…ô to animar~…ĺs, so as to ¶aus…ô tńß…ôm to b…ô *…ô ¶tors for tńß…ô transf…ôr of por~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôn. aft…ôr f…ôrtir~…ĺization, tńß…ô o*arj of tńß…ô fr~…ĺow…ôr d…ô*…ôr~…ĺops into fruit ¶ontainiŇč s…ô…ôds.
S.FORM: == balu == ŤäĪ manamuh kaku tu kalubalu amin. siwkay nu balu balu sa , mahiza ngangan nu Lipun "hana". yadah ku kulit nu balu, sisa bangcal ku balu. maydih kami tu balu. u Sakizaya a lisin sa, izaw ku "Baluinana", sapibetik tu balu, u mapalaway a lisin kuyza. anu patuduay micumud tu mapalaway a selal atu cacudadan , misaungay tu ku Baluinana a lisin. anu sibaluay a kakitizaan, tayni tu ku wadu atu adipapang. tinaku a kamu balubaluan ŤäĪŚúíÔľõŤäĪśĶ∑ balubaluan ku umah nu Maibul. kasibaluan ťĖčŤäĪÁöĄŚ≠£ÁĮÄ kasibaluan nu mami ayza a bulad. misabalu ŚźęŤčěŚĺÖśĒĺ misabalu tu ku mami. sibalu śúČŤäĪ sibalu tu ku bicacay. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A flower, sometimes known as a bloom or blossom, is the reproductive structure found in plants that are floral (plants of the division Magnoliophyta, also called angiosperms). The biological function of a flower is to effect reproduction, usually by providing a mechanism for the union of sperm with eggs. Flowers may facilitate outcrossing or allow selfing . Some flowers produce diaspores without fertilization . Flowers contain sporangia and are the site where gametophytes develop. Many flowers have evolved to be attractive to animals, so as to cause them to be vectors for the transfer of pollen. After fertilization, the ovary of the flower develops into fruit containing seeds.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺu == * manamuńß kaku tu kar~…ĺubar~…ĺu amin. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺu bar~…ĺu sa , mańßiza ŇčaŇčan nu r~…ĺipun "ńßana". jadańß ku kur~…ĺit nu bar~…ĺu, sisa baŇč ¶ar~…ĺ ku bar~…ĺu. majdińß kami tu bar~…ĺu. u sakizaja a r~…ĺisin sa, izaw ku "bar~…ĺuinana", sapib…ôtik tu bar~…ĺu, u mapar~…ĺawaj a r~…ĺisin kujza. anu patuduaj mi ¶umud tu mapar~…ĺawaj a s…ôr~…ĺar~…ĺ atu ¶a ¶udadan , misauŇčaj tu ku bar~…ĺuinana a r~…ĺisin. anu sibar~…ĺuaj a kakitizaan, tajni tu ku wadu atu adipapaŇč. tinaku a kamu bar~…ĺubar~…ĺuan ***** bar~…ĺubar~…ĺuan ku umańß nu maibur~…ĺ. kasibar~…ĺuan ***** kasibar~…ĺuan nu mami ajza a bur~…ĺad. misabar~…ĺu **** misabar~…ĺu tu ku mami. sibar~…ĺu ** sibar~…ĺu tu ku bi ¶a ¶aj. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a fr~…ĺow…ôr, som…ôtim…ôs known as a br~…ĺoom or br~…ĺossom, is tńß…ô r…ôprodu ¶ti*…ô stru ¶tur…ô found in pr~…ĺants tńßat ar…ô fr~…ĺorar~…ĺ (pr~…ĺants of tńß…ô di*ision magnor~…ĺiopńßjta, ar~…ĺso ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd aŇčiosp…ôrms). tńß…ô bior~…ĺogi ¶ar~…ĺ fun ¶tion of a fr~…ĺow…ôr is to …ôff…ô ¶t r…ôprodu ¶tion, usuar~…ĺr~…ĺj bj pro*idiŇč a m…ô ¶ńßanism for tńß…ô union of sp…ôrm witńß …ôggs. fr~…ĺow…ôrs maj fa ¶ir~…ĺitat…ô out ¶rossiŇč or ar~…ĺr~…ĺow s…ôr~…ĺfiŇč . som…ô fr~…ĺow…ôrs produ ¶…ô diaspor…ôs witńßout f…ôrtir~…ĺization . fr~…ĺow…ôrs ¶ontain sporaŇčia and ar…ô tńß…ô sit…ô wńß…ôr…ô gam…ôtopńßjt…ôs d…ô*…ôr~…ĺop. manj fr~…ĺow…ôrs ńßa*…ô …ô*or~…ĺ*…ôd to b…ô attra ¶ti*…ô to animar~…ĺs, so as to ¶aus…ô tńß…ôm to b…ô *…ô ¶tors for tńß…ô transf…ôr of por~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôn. aft…ôr f…ôrtir~…ĺization, tńß…ô o*arj of tńß…ô fr~…ĺow…ôr d…ô*…ôr~…ĺops into fruit ¶ontainiŇč s…ô…ôds.
